rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash for description as it accurately includes 'L. S: D:' visible on the page as column headers. gpt-5-mini omitted the column labels. Confirmed visually.

Description from gemini-flash ('Recd Mr Hellen Plate Money') is visually more accurate than gpt-5-mini's ('Reeived Mr Hallins Plate Money'). Amounts were a point of disagreement (8 pounds vs 8 shillings); visual verification clearly shows '0 8 0', meaning 8 shillings. This combines the best description with the visually verified amount.
rows.english_description: Plate money received from Mr Hellen.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Plate money received from Mr Hellen, amounting to £0 8s 0d.

rows.description: Recd of Mr Webber Burser Library Midsumr 1751.
rows.notes: Description from gemini-flash ('Recd of Mr Webber Burser Library Midsumr 1751.') is visually more accurate than gpt-5-mini's ('Received of Mr Webber Bursar Library Monies May 1751'), especially for 'Recd' and 'Midsumr'. Amounts agreed and confirmed visually as 0 pounds, 7 shillings, 6 pence.
rows.english_description: Library payment received from Mr Webber (the bursar) at Midsummer 1751.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Library payment received from Mr Webber (the bursar) at Midsummer 1751, amounting to £0 7s 6d.

rows.description: Recd by Bill Drawn on W Smith, by ordr of
rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash's interpretation for this row. The description is clearly visible, and no amounts are recorded on this physical line, which is the first part of a two-line entry. gpt-5-mini merged this description with the next physical row into a single entry, which does not accurately reflect the physical layout.
rows.english_description: Receipt by a bill drawn on W. Smith by order of an unspecified party (entry incomplete).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Receipt by a bill drawn on W. Smith by order of an unspecified party; no amount recorded.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash's extraction for this row. The description 'Mr Bendysshe, payable last Midsummer' is clear and contains the associated amounts (100 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence). This is the second line of a two-line entry, following the structure identified by gemini-flash.
rows.english_description: Payment due from Mr Bendysshe that was payable at the previous Midsummer.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment due from Mr Bendysshe that was payable at the previous Midsummer, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.description: Recd by Bill Ditto payable last Xmas
rows.notes: Description from gemini-flash ('Recd by Bill Ditto payable last Xmas') is visually more accurate, especially 'Recd' and 'Xmas' which gpt-5-mini rendered as 'Received' and 'Janr' respectively. Amounts agreed and confirmed as 100 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence.
rows.english_description: Amount received by a bill (as above) payable at the previous Christmas.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Amount received by a bill drawn on W. Smith, payable at the previous Christmas, amounting to £100 0s 0d.

rows.description: Recd Busson Library Monry Mids: ending 1749.
rows.notes: Description from gemini-flash ('Recd Busson Library Monry Mids: ending 1749.') is visually accurate, including the specific transcription of 'Monry' (not 'Money') and 'Busson' (not 'Brown'). Amounts agreed and confirmed as 2 pounds, 12 shillings, 6 pence.
rows.english_description: Library money received from Busson at Midsummer for the year ending 1749.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Library money received from Busson at Midsummer for the year ending 1749, amounting to £2 12s 6d.

rows.description: Recd Ditto Mids: Half Year ending 1748.
rows.notes: Description from gemini-flash ('Recd Ditto Mids: Half Year ending 1748.') is visually more accurate, particularly 'Recd' and the capitalization of 'Half Year'. Amounts agreed and confirmed as 2 pounds, 17 shillings, 6 pence.
rows.english_description: Half-year library payment from Busson for the half-year ending 1748.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-year library payment from Busson for the half-year ending 1748, amounting to £2 17s 6d.

rows.notes: Description from gemini-flash ('Recd Mr Northover Plate Money') is visually more accurate than gpt-5-mini's ('Received Mr Northmore Plate Money'), especially 'Recd' and 'Northover' (not 'Northmore'). Amounts were a point of disagreement (8 pounds vs 8 shillings); visual verification clearly shows '0 8 0', meaning 8 shillings.
rows.english_description: Plate money received from Mr Northover.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Plate money received from Mr Northover, amounting to £0 8s 0d.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash for amounts. Visual inspection confirms 221 pounds, 17 shillings, and 6 pence. The pence digit is clearly a '6', not an '8' as extracted by gpt-5-mini. The note from gpt-5-mini about faintness applied to the pounds digit, not the pence.

rows.description: Debs: praecedentu anni c the computator
rows.notes: Description from gemini-flash ('Debs: praecedentu anni c the computator') is visually more accurate than gpt-5-mini's ('Deb. praesenti anni &c pro computatus'). Amounts agreed and confirmed visually as 168 pounds, 4 shillings, 9 pence. No significant ambiguity found in the pounds digit, contrary to gpt-5-mini's note.
rows.english_description: Debts of the preceding year as entered by the computator (accountant).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Debts of the preceding year as entered by the computator, amounting to £168 4s 9d.

rows.notes: Selected gemini-flash for its correct interpretation of the half-penny symbol. Visual inspection confirms 169 pounds, 6 shillings, 10 pence, and a clear trailing loop/flourish (resembling 'ob' or a cursive 'd') which is interpreted as 0.5 pence. gpt-5-mini noted a flourish but did not interpret it as a monetary value. The pounds digit '9' is clear, contrary to gpt-5-mini's note.

Selected gemini-flash for its correct interpretation of the farthing symbol. Visual inspection confirms 333 pounds, 14 shillings, 6 pence, and a clear 'qd' symbol interpreted as 0.25 pence. gpt-5-mini missed this symbol entirely.
Full consensus between extractors on all values (description, amounts, and pence fraction). Values confirmed visually as 893 pounds, 3 shillings, 8 pence, and 0.5 pence (half-penny).
